subscribe(listener).notes: Fires synchronously on every state mutation with the latest snapshot.

params.action: { type: 'deal' } | { type: 'hit' } | { type: 'stand' } | { type: 'double' } | { type: 'split' } | { type: 'next' }

dispatch(action).returns: void  // illegal actions are noops; no exceptions thrown into UI

shape.dealerHand: Array<{ card: Card, visible: bool }>  // hole card has visible=false until dealer_turn
shape.playerHands: Array<{ cards: Array<Card>, isActive: bool, outcome: 'player_blackjack'|'player_win'|'push'|'dealer_win'|'player_bust'|null, bet: number }>
shape.legalActions: Array<'hit'|'stand'|'double'|'split'>  // for the active hand only; empty when phase != 'player_turn'
shape.decisionLog: Array<{ playerHandSnapshot, dealerUpcard, chosenAction, correctAction }>  // populated through the hand; consumed by hint-and-review on resolve

notes: ui-render is a pure render-on-state subscriber. It MUST NOT mutate state directly; all user input flows back through dispatch(). The legalActions array in the snapshot is the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for action-button enabled/disabled state — ui-render must not re-derive legality. Phase 'resolved' is the state where the Deal/Next button must be enabled (per S4.A3). Phase 'betting' is also a dealable state. dispatch({type:'next'}) returns the machine to a dealable phase without page reload.
